translations.en: She went out searching until she passed some youths of the Children of Israel who asked where she was going. She said she sought her son Yahya, for the Fire was mentioned and he went away in a daze. They went with her until they met a shepherd. She described her son, and the shepherd said: 'I left him at a mountain pass, his feet soaking in water, his gaze on the sky, saying: By Your glory, my Master! I will not taste a cold drink until I see my station with You.' When his mother found him, she took his head to her chest and adjured him to come home. He went with her. She asked him to change to wool, which was softer. He did. She cooked lentils for him; he ate, was full, and slept. Sleep overcame him and he missed his prayer. A voice called in his sleep: 'O Yahya! Do you want an abode better than My abode and a proximity better than My proximity?' He awoke and said: 'O Lord! Forgive my slip. By Your glory, I will not take shade except under Bayt al-Maqdis.'
